contents.value: **if** — FreeMarker keyword.
**else** — FreeMarker keyword.
**elseif** — FreeMarker keyword.
**list** — FreeMarker keyword.
**as** — FreeMarker keyword.
**?string** — Built-in function/command in FreeMarker.
**?number** — Built-in function/command in FreeMarker.
**?c** — Built-in function/command in FreeMarker.
**?html** — Built-in function/command in FreeMarker.
**?xml** — Built-in function/command in FreeMarker.

An **HTML element** consists of a start tag, content, and end tag.
```html
<tag>content</tag>
```

An **attribute** provides additional information about an element.
```html
<tag attr="value">
```

A **CSS selector** targets HTML elements for styling.
```css
.class, #id, tag { property: value; }
```

An **event** fires when user interaction or browser activity occurs.
```
click, keydown, load, submit, resize
```
The **Document Object Model** represents the HTML page as a tree of nodes.

The **viewport** is the visible area of a web page in the browser window.
```html
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width">
```
**Responsive design** adapts layouts to different screen sizes using media queries and flexible units.

**Flexbox** is a CSS layout model for distributing space along a single axis.
```css
display: flex; justify-content: center;
```

**CSS Grid** is a two-dimensional layout system.
```css
display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 2fr;
```

A **pseudo-class** selects elements in a specific state.
```css
a:hover, input:focus, li:nth-child(2)
```

CSS **custom properties** (variables) store reusable values.
```css
:root { --color: blue; } .el { color: var(--color); }
```
**Specificity** determines which CSS rule applies when multiple rules target the same element. Inline > ID > Class > Tag.

```freemarker2
expr?builtin_name
```
FreeMarker string built-ins provide powerful string manipulation capabilities applied using the ?suffix syntax on string expressions. They cover case conversion, trimming, searching, replacing, splitting, and formatting — enabling rich text processing directly within templates without Java code.

```freemarker2
sequence?builtin_name
```
FreeMarker sequence built-ins operate on ordered collections (arrays/lists) and provide operations for querying size, sorting, reversing, joining, chunking, and filtering sequences. Sequences are zero-indexed and support random access by index.

```freemarker2
hash?builtin_name
```
FreeMarker hash built-ins operate on hash (map/dictionary) values and provide access to the keys and values collections. Hashes in FreeMarker map string keys to arbitrary values and are the primary structure for data model objects passed from the Java backend.

```freemarker2
number?builtin_name
```
FreeMarker number built-ins provide formatting and conversion operations for numeric values including rounding, absolute value, and string conversion with locale-aware formatting. Numbers in FreeMarker automatically handle both integers and floating-point values.

```freemarker2
<#if condition>...<#elseif condition>...<#else>...</#if>
```
The conditional directive that renders its enclosed content only when the condition evaluates to true. Supports optional elseif and else branches for multi-way branching. Conditions can use comparison operators (==, !=, <, >, <=, >=), logical operators (&&, ||, !), and built-in tests.

```freemarker2
<#list sequence as item>...<#sep>separator</#sep></#list>
```
Iterates over a sequence or collection, rendering the body once for each element. The item variable holds the current element. Supports <#sep> for inter-element separators, <#items> for explicit item blocks, and <#else> for empty collection handling. Tracks iteration via item?index, item?counter, and item?is_last.

```freemarker2
<#assign name = value> | <#assign name>capture</#assign>
```
Creates or replaces a variable in the current template namespace. The simple form assigns an expression value directly. The capture form captures all output between the tags as a string value, useful for building complex strings from template output.

```freemarker2
<#macro name param1 param2=default>body</#macro>
```
Defines a reusable template fragment (macro) with optional parameters and default values. Macros are called with the <@name> syntax and support nested content via <#nested>. They enable DRY template design by encapsulating repeated layout patterns.

```freemarker2
<#assign name=value>
```
Creates or replaces a variable in the current namespace.

```freemarker2
<#include path>
```
Processes a specified template and includes the output.

```freemarker2
<#macro name param1 param2>...</#macro>
```
Defines a macro (reusable template fragment) with parameters.

```freemarker2
<#function name param1 param2>...<#return value></#function>
```
Defines a function that returns a value.

```freemarker2
<#switch value><#case refValue>...<#break><#default>...</#switch>
```
Multi-branch selection based on value comparison.

```freemarker2
<#attempt>...<#recover>...</#attempt>
```
Error handling. Renders recover block if attempt fails.

```freemarker2
<#setting name=value>
```
Sets FreeMarker engine settings (locale, number_format, date_format, etc.).

```freemarker2
${expression}
```
Outputs the value of an expression. The primary output mechanism.

```freemarker2
sequence?size
```
Returns the number of elements in a sequence or hash.

```freemarker2
value?has_content
```
Returns true if the value is not null, not empty, and not a blank string.

```freemarker2
string?upper_case
```
Converts the string to upper case.

```freemarker2
string?lower_case
```
Converts the string to lower case.

```freemarker2
string?trim
```
Removes leading and trailing whitespace.

```freemarker2
string?replace(what, with)
```
Replaces all occurrences of a substring.

```freemarker2
string?split(separator)
```
Splits a string into a sequence by separator.

```freemarker2
string?starts_with(prefix)
```
Returns true if the string starts with the given prefix.

```freemarker2
string?contains(substring)
```
Returns true if the string contains the given substring.

```freemarker2
string?index_of(substring)
```
Returns the index of the first occurrence of substring, or -1.

```freemarker2
string?length
```
Returns the length of the string.

```freemarker2
sequence?first
```
Returns the first element of a sequence.

```freemarker2
sequence?last
```
Returns the last element of a sequence.

```freemarker2
sequence?sort_by(key)
```
Returns the sequence sorted by the specified sub-variable.

```freemarker2
sequence?filter(item -> condition)
```
Returns a new sequence containing only elements for which the condition is true.

```freemarker2
sequence?map(item -> expression)
```
Returns a new sequence where each element is the result of the expression.

```freemarker2
sequence?join(separator)
```
Concatenates elements of a sequence into a string with the given separator.

```freemarker2
hash?keys
```
Returns a sequence containing all keys of the hash.

```freemarker2
hash?values
```
Returns a sequence containing all values of the hash.

```freemarker2
value?date
```
Converts a value to a date.

```freemarker2
value?datetime
```
Converts a value to a date-time.

```freemarker2
string?url
```
URL-encodes the string.

```freemarker2
string?j_string
```
Escapes the string for use in a Java or JavaScript string literal.

```freemarker2
string?json_string
```
Escapes the string for use in a JSON string value.

```freemarker2
value?is_string
```
Returns true if the value is a string.

```freemarker2
value?is_number
```
Returns true if the value is a number.

```freemarker2
value?is_boolean
```
Returns true if the value is a boolean.

```freemarker2
value?is_sequence
```
Returns true if the value is a sequence.

```freemarker2
value?is_hash
```
Returns true if the value is a hash.

```freemarker2
value??
```
Missing value test operator. Returns true if the variable exists and is not null.

```freemarker2
value!default
```
Default value operator. Returns default if value is missing.
